expectations.text: Covers all pension pillars: AFP, OTP, IPS, and folkepensjon
expectations.evidence: Four dedicated sections: 'Pillar 1: Folkepensjon', 'Pillar 2: AFP', 'Pillar 3: OTP from KM', 'Pillar 4: IPS'. Each with eligibility rules, projections, and after-tax amounts.

expectations.text: Explains AFP eligibility rules and notes requirement for 7 of last 9 years in AFP-covered employment before 62
expectations.evidence: 'Must have been in AFP-covered employment for 7 of the last 9 years before turning 62.' Also flags strategic implication: 'staying at KM (or another AFP employer) until at least age ~55 before any full-time entrepreneurial switch.'

expectations.text: Notes IPS max increases to 25,000 kr from 2026 with 5,500 kr tax deduction
expectations.evidence: Screening table: 'IPS maximum increased to 25,000 kr/year (from 15,000)... Effective from 2026 tax year... High — you are leaving 10,000 kr of contribution room unused.' Tax deduction: '22% of contribution = 5,500 kr/year (at 25k).'

expectations.text: Projects OTP accumulation based on typical employer contribution rates
expectations.evidence: Models both 5% and 7% employer rates. At 5%: ~68,800/yr → 3.7M at 62. At 7%: ~96,300/yr → 5.2M at 62. Contribution base calculated: '1,500,000 - 124,028 (1G) = 1,375,972 kr.'

expectations.text: Models pension income at multiple ages (at least 62, 65, and 67)
expectations.evidence: Combined projection table at ages 62, 65, 67, and 72 (with two drawdown scenarios at 72). After-tax totals range from 525-711k at 62 to 570-761k at 67.

expectations.text: Recommends maximizing IPS contribution with clear reasoning (tax deduction + formuesskatt exemption)
expectations.evidence: Option A (recommended): 'Maximize IPS to 25,000 kr/year.' Reasoning: immediate tax deduction (5,500/yr), formuesskatt exemption, compounding benefit, and: 'it is an interest-free loan from the state.' Net cost: 7,800 kr/year.

expectations.text: Connects pension to FI: explains that expected pension income reduces the required FI portfolio size
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Pension as FI Accelerator' section. FI without pension: 17.1M. FI with pension at 62: 2.1M (or 0 if pension covers expenses). Introduces 'bridge portfolio' concept: 'build a bridge portfolio to cover ages 50-62, pension takes over from there.'
All pension projection tables labeled 'After Tax'. Combined table explicitly shows after-tax totals. Tax rates noted: '~25-30% effective pension tax rate.'

claims.text: Read vault notes and discovered user is living month-to-month (Personal Finance Plan), positioned at Level 1 on freedom ladder
Flagged AFP eligibility as the biggest strategic risk given GTB Ventures entrepreneurial ambitions from OKRs
Introduced bridge portfolio concept: pension covers 62+, portfolio only needs to bridge gap from early retirement to 62
Connected IPS recommendation to Q4 tax review and norwegian-tax-expert skill delegation

user_notes_summary: Web search unavailable; FI tracker doesn't exist; expense baseline not established; KM OTP rate unknown; AFP reform risk noted; Obsidian CLI failed.
